cases.description: Telehealth visit with only a reported home BP: every unmeasured vital must be null, not invented
Ambiguous dose (25 vs 50 mg, records pending): must land in extraction_notes and must not be resolved by guessing

checks.name: metoprolol dose is not silently guessed as exactly 25 or exactly 50
checks.expected: metoprolol present with dose null or explicitly uncertain (not a single guessed value)
checks.actual: metoprolol 25 mg or 50 mg twice daily [unknown]

checks.name: the dose ambiguity lands in extraction_notes
checks.actual: "Metoprolol dose uncertain—patient reports either 25 mg or 50 mg twice daily; pharmacy records requested and pending ; Diuretic agent not specified; patient referred to as 'water pill' without confirmation of specific agent or dose ; Atorvastatin 20 mg nightly confirmed from bottle patient brought in ; Temperature, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, and weight not recorded in note ; Route of administration not specified for any medication"

checks.name: confirmed atorvastatin 20 mg nightly extracted normally

cases.description: Bundled renewal pair — 8 planted landlord-favoring changes: rent +$145, auto-renewal added, water shifted to tenant, lawn care shifted to tenant, guest limit halved, notice 30→60 days, new pet fee, new early-termination fee

checks.name: finds the rent increase to $1,595 (rent_and_deposits, favors landlord)
finds the new automatic-renewal clause (term_and_renewal, favors landlord)
finds water shifted from landlord to tenant (utilities, favors landlord)
checks.actual: "Tenant now pays for water" [utilities, favors_landlord, medium]

checks.name: finds lawn care shifted to tenant (maintenance, favors landlord)
checks.actual: "Tenant now responsible for lawn care" [maintenance_and_repairs, favors_landlord, medium]

checks.name: finds the guest limit cut from 14 to 7 days (rules_and_use, favors landlord)
checks.actual: "Maximum guest stay cut in half" [rules_and_use, favors_landlord, low]

checks.name: finds notice to vacate extended 30→60 days (favors landlord)
finds the new monthly pet fee (fees)
finds the new early-termination fee (favors landlord)
checks.expected: new early-termination fee of two months' rent

cases.description: Bundled month-to-month pair — 7 planted concepts including two tenant-favorable changes that must not be mislabeled: term conversion, +$50 premium, late grace 3→5 days (favors tenant), deposit return 21→14 days (favors tenant), termination rework, entry notice 48→24h, new insurance requirement

checks.name: finds the fixed-term → month-to-month conversion (term_and_renewal)
checks.actual: "Fixed 12-month term converted to month-to-month tenancy" [term_and_renewal, unclear, high]

checks.name: BIAS CHECK: late-fee grace 3→5 days is favors_tenant
checks.expected: late-fee grace period lengthened, judged favors_tenant
checks.actual: "Late fee grace period extended" [fees, favors_tenant, low]

checks.name: BIAS CHECK: deposit return 21→14 days is favors_tenant
checks.expected: deposit return window shortened, judged favors_tenant
checks.actual: "Security deposit return period shortened" [rent_and_deposits, favors_tenant, medium]

checks.name: finds the lease-break penalty removal (favors_tenant, or unclear when merged with the landlord's new termination right)
checks.expected: lease-break penalty removed (favors_tenant alone, or unclear when merged)
checks.actual: "Landlord gains ability to terminate at will; lease-break penalty removed" [termination, unclear, high]

checks.name: finds the landlord's new right to terminate at will (favors landlord)
checks.expected: landlord can now terminate with 30 days' notice
checks.actual: "Fixed 12-month term converted to month-to-month tenancy" [term_and_renewal, unclear, high]

checks.name: finds entry notice cut 48→24 hours (entry_and_privacy, favors landlord)
checks.actual: "Landlord entry notice period shortened" [entry_and_privacy, favors_landlord, medium]

checks.name: finds the new renter's-insurance requirement (favors landlord)
no invented changes (8 planted counting the termination rework as two, ≤ 9 reported)

cases.description: False-positive restraint pair — the maintenance clause is reworded with identical meaning (must NOT be reported); the late fee quietly rises $50→$75 inside similar rewording (must be reported)

checks.name: finds the late fee increase to $75 buried in the rewording (favors landlord)
checks.actual: "Late fee increased from $50 to $75" [fees, favors_landlord, medium]

checks.name: does not report the identically-meaning maintenance rewording as substantive
checks.expected: no entry treating the reworded maintenance/cleanliness clause as a substantive change

checks.name: every source excerpt appears verbatim in the lease
checks.expected: no entry whose source_excerpt does not appear verbatim in the document

cases.description: A soup recipe — must be rejected as not an obligation document, with no events
A Vue 2-era stack — deprecated request, 2-majors-behind vue-router, patch-behind lodash, and peer conflicts pointing at vue: tiers, alerts, and sequencing all at once

checks.name: HEADLINE: no invented versions (post-validation stripped nothing)
checks.expected: validation_warnings empty (no invented packages or versions)

checks.name: request appears in deprecated_alerts
checks.actual: request@2.79.0 is deprecated and end-of-life per https://github.com/request/request/issues/3142. No further security patches are available. Recommended replacement: axios (drop-in HTTP client), node-fetch, or native fetch/http modules. Plan migration as soon as feasible after wave 3 verification.

checks.name: peer conflicts yield sequencing guidance
checks.actual: ["Vuex 4.1.0 requires vue ^3.2.0, but declared range is ^2.6.10 — resolved by upgrading vue and vuex together in Wave 1, where vue@3.5.39 satisfies the requirement.","Vue Router 5.1.0 requires vue ^3.5.34, but declared range is ^2.6.10 — resolved by upgrading vue and vue-router together in Wave 1, where vue@3.5.39 satisfies the requirement."]

checks.name: vuex is never sequenced before vue (its peer requirement)
checks.expected: vue (wave n) upgraded no later than vuex (wave ≥ n)

checks.name: current typescript is in already_current, not a wave
checks.expected: typescript in already_current and in no wave

cases.description: vue + vue-router + vuex all major-behind — the tightly coupled framework stack must migrate as one wave

checks.name: HEADLINE: no invented versions
checks.expected: validation_warnings empty (no invented packages or versions)

checks.name: vue, vue-router, and vuex share a wave
checks.expected: vue, vue-router, vuex grouped in the same wave
checks.actual: vue: wave 1, vue-router: wave 1, vuex: wave 1

checks.name: current axios stays out of the waves

cases.description: A fully current manifest — the honest quiet case: zero waves, zero plans, everything in already_current, no padding

checks.name: all four packages listed already_current
checks.expected: express, cors, helmet, zod all in already_current

checks.name: no phantom deprecation or conflict guidance

cases.description: Two small bumps — both safe_now, groupable in one quick wave

nothing is tiered breaking_likely

cases.description: A deprecated (but nearly current) moment — deprecation must dominate the tier and produce an alert

checks.name: HEADLINE: no invented versions
checks.expected: validation_warnings empty (no invented packages or versions)

checks.name: deprecated moment is tiered breaking_likely despite being only a minor behind
moment appears in deprecated_alerts
checks.actual: moment (^2.29.1) is deprecated and in maintenance mode. The project recommends migrating to Luxon, Day.js, or date-fns. Upgrading to 2.30.1 is a short-term patch; plan a full replacement as part of future technical debt work.

cases.description: react + react-dom three majors behind with a lockstep peer requirement, plus an unrelated patch bump

checks.name: HEADLINE: no invented versions
checks.expected: validation_warnings empty (no invented packages or versions)

checks.name: react-dom is never sequenced before react
